title: Hardening: staleness automation vs. annual update points, expanded red-team, a11y/PDF/privacy verification + pilot runbook
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task hardens the system for pilot readiness: staleness re-verification automation tied to the real update points (HHS poverty guidelines in January; SNAP COLA in October), an expanded red-team, end-to-end accessibility/PDF/privacy verification, and a pilot onboarding/distribution runbook for libraries/food banks/navigators.
objective: Harden the system for pilot readiness with staleness automation tied to real update points, an expanded red-team, end-to-end a11y/PDF/privacy verification, and a pilot runbook.
acceptanceCriteria: Staleness re-verification automation tied to the real update points (HHS poverty guidelines in January; SNAP COLA in October) verified
Expanded red-team still 100% refused/redirected; WCAG 2.2 AA + offline PDF + zero-PII verified end-to-end
Pilot onboarding + distribution runbook written (for libraries/food banks/navigators)
